vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1433

plan.title: v0.39.2 vendored refresh aborts: extractor treats GitHub tarball pax_global_header as content root; README still says installer clones

narratives.Description: v0.39.2 vendored refresh aborts: extractor treats GitHub tarball pax_global_header as content root; README still says installer clones
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

Testing the new **v0.39.2** vendored-refresh path (the #1425 fix) on a real vendored (no-`.git`) install: **detection and the git-safety guardrail now work, but the upgrade aborts during tarball extraction.** Two related defects from the same incompletely-finished work, in one issue:

1. **Bug (Upgrade Blocker):** `extractCoreTarball` mis-identifies the content root as `pax_global_header`, so every vendored refresh fails.
2. **Docs:** the README still says the installer "clones deft" with no mention of the vendored / git-free file-swap path that v0.39.2 now implements.

## 1. Extraction bug -- `cmd/deft-install/upgrade.go` (@ v0.39.2)

### Repro
Vendored install (`.deft/core/` committed into the project, no `.git/` of its own):
```
deft-install --yes --upgrade --repo-root . --json
```
Output (exit 1):
```
Detected a vendored framework payload at ...\.deft\core (no .git of its own).
Refreshing via git-free file swap -- the installer will NOT run git against your project repo ...
Error: vendored refresh: could not extract tarball: tarball content root "pax_global_header" missing after extract
```
Good news: detection (L252) and the git-safety guardrail (L253) are correct, and the atomic swap rolled back cleanly -- `.deft/core` untouched, no `.bak`/temp left. Only extraction fails.

### Root cause
GitHub source tarballs (`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/tarball/{ref}`) begin with a global PAX header entry: `hdr.Name == "pax_global_header"`, `hdr.Typeflag == tar.TypeXGlobalHeader` (`'g'`). Go's `tar.Reader.Next()` returns that entry. `extractCoreTarball` captures `rootName` from the **first** entry it sees, *before* inspecting the typeflag:

`cmd/deft-install/upgrade.go` (v0.39.2):
- **L376-378** -- rootName taken from the first entry unconditionally:
  ```go
  if rootName == "" {
      rootName = parts[0]   // == "pax_global_header" for GitHub tarballs
  }
  ```
- The `switch hdr.Typeflag` only skips the header for *extraction* (**L425-428**, `default: continue`) -- too late; `rootName` was already set at L377.
- **L432-438** then computes `contentRoot = destDir/pax_global_header` and `os.Stat` fails -> **L437** `tarball content root %q missing after extract`.

Real content extracts fine under `deftai-directive-<sha>/`, but `rootName` is stuck at `pax_global_header`.

### Fix
Skip PAX global/extended header entries before computing `rootName`. After the `name == ""` check (~L374), add:
```go
if hdr.Typeflag == tar.TypeXGlobalHeader || hdr.Typeflag == tar.TypeXHeader {
    continue
}
```
(Equivalently: only set `rootName` for `TypeDir`/`TypeReg` entries.) The webinstaller's TS extractor (`deftai/webinstaller` `src/lib/bootstrap/emitDeftCore.ts`) is immune because it derives the top level per-entry (`stripTopLevel`) rather than trusting the first entry.

### Test
Add a fixture tarball that begins with a `pax_global_header` (mirrors GitHub's `/tarball` output) and assert `extractCoreTarball` returns the `deftai-directive-<sha>/` root, not the header.

## 2. Docs -- README still says the installer "clones"

README "Getting Started" still describes only the clone model:
> "The installer ... installs git if needed, **clones deft**, wires it into your project's `AGENTS.md` ..."

and the Canonical-path banner frames `deft-install --upgrade` purely as a payload refresh without noting that there are **two on-disk layouts**. As of v0.39.2 the installer also supports a **vendored (no-`.git`) payload** refreshed via git-free file swap. README should document both origins -- binary (clone, with `.git`) vs webinstaller (vendored, no `.git`) -- and that `--upgrade` detects the layout, refreshes accordingly, and never runs git against the consumer repo.

## Related
- #1425 -- vendored-install upgrade support + safety guardrail. This is the direct follow-up: detection + guardrail landed in v0.39.2, but extraction regressed and the docs were not updated.
- #1411 / #1413 -- "stuck on old version" banner + release-notes guidance.
- deftai/webinstaller#217 -- handoff prompt should point agents at the remote README/UPGRADING.

Evidence: tested with `deft-install v0.39.2` on a vendored `.deft/core` (no `.git`); `upgrade.go` read at tag `v0.39.2`.
narratives.Problem: On a real vendored (no-.git) install, deft-install --upgrade aborts during extraction with 'tarball content root "pax_global_header" missing after extract'. extractCoreTarball captures rootName from the FIRST tar entry, which for GitHub source tarballs is the global PAX header (tar.TypeXGlobalHeader), so the content root resolves to pax_global_header instead of the deftai-directive-<sha> wrapper dir. Separately, the README still describes only the clone install model and never documents the v0.39.2 vendored git-free file-swap path.
narratives.Action: Skip PAX global/extended header entries (tar.TypeXGlobalHeader / tar.TypeXHeader) before resolving rootName in extractCoreTarball, add a regression test using a tarball that begins with a pax_global_header, and update the README to document both on-disk layouts (clone with .git vs vendored without .git) and the layout-aware, git-safe --upgrade behaviour.
narratives.Test: go test ./cmd/deft-install/... (new regression test fails before the fix, passes after) + gofmt clean; task verify:links for the README change; full task ci:local during the release pre-flight.

items.title: Fix extractCoreTarball to skip PAX global/extended header entries (tar.TypeXGlobalHeader/TypeXHeader) before resolving rootName, so the GitHub tarball wrapper dir becomes the content root (#1433)
Add a regression test that builds a tarball beginning with a pax_global_header and asserts extractCoreTarball returns the deftai-directive-<sha> wrapper root, not pax_global_header
Update README to document vendored (git-free, no .git) vs clone install layouts and that deft-install --upgrade detects the layout and never runs git against the consumer repo
Add CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entries for the extractor fix (Fixed) and the README docs change (Changed)

references.title: Issue #1433: v0.39.2 vendored refresh aborts: extractor treats GitHub tarball pax_global_header as content root; README still says installer clones
